expectations.evidence: session-brief.md shows '[LOADED:woocommerce:10.x] - from wcAnalytics JS global, version: 10.7.0-rc.1'. eval-report.md confirms 'Tech Pack ID: LOADED:woocommerce:10.x'. The tech pack load is substantiated by the detection source (wcAnalytics.woo_version JS global).

expectations.text: At least one of /wp-json/wc/store/v1/products or /wp-json/wc/v3/products probed
expectations.evidence: session-brief.md endpoint table shows both '/wp-json/wc/store/v1/products' (GET, None auth, Phase 5, 'Product listing - working') and '/wp-json/wc/v3/products' (GET, 401 auth required, Phase 5). eval-report.md confirms store/v1/products 'returned 5 products' and wc/v3/products returned 401.

expectations.text: api-surfaces/ directory contains at least one file
expectations.evidence: outputs/docs/research/woocommerce-com-woocommerce-demo/api-surfaces/woocommerce-store-api.md exists. File is present in the directory listing.

claims.evidence: session-brief.md states 'WordPress 6.9.4 + WooCommerce 10.7.0-rc.1' sourced from wcAnalytics.woo_version JS global — a reliable in-page signal.

claims.claim: /woocommerce-demo/ returns 404, so target is actually the main WooCommerce.com site
claims.evidence: session-brief.md Note and eval-report.md both state the /woocommerce-demo/ path returns 404 and the actual analysis was of woocommerce.com marketplace. The slug 'woocommerce-com-woocommerce-demo' reflects the attempted target path.
session-brief.md endpoint table includes '/?wc-ajax=get_refreshed_fragments' returning 'Cart fragments - working' in Phase 5.

needs_review: The /woocommerce-demo/ 404 means the eval prompt's target URL redirected to the main site — the WooCommerce version detected (10.x) may differ from what would be on an actual demo store if one existed.

suggestions.assertion: api-surfaces/ directory contains at least one file
suggestions.reason: This assertion only checks existence, not content. A 0-byte woocommerce-store-api.md would still pass. Consider asserting that the file documents at least one endpoint with HTTP method, path, and auth requirement.

No assertion checks that wc-ajax endpoints were probed (only /wp-json routes). The wc-ajax=get_refreshed_fragments endpoint is a distinct WooCommerce surface that an old skill might miss — it would be worth adding as an assertion.

eval_feedback.overall: The three assertions effectively catch tech pack loading and API probing. Adding content-depth verification for api-surfaces/ would strengthen the eval.
